title: Identify the Known Values
text: Start by identifying the known values from the problem statement. What is the initial velocity, final velocity, and distance traveled?

title: Use the Kinematic Equation
text: Recall the kinematic equation that relates initial velocity, final velocity, acceleration, and distance: $${v_f}^2={v_i}^2+2a d$$. Which values do you need to substitute into this equation?

title: Substitute Known Values
text: Substitute the known values into the equation: $${65}^2=0^2+2a\times0.25$$. Solve for acceleration (a).

title: Solve for Acceleration
text: Rearrange the equation to solve for acceleration: $$a=\frac{{v_f}^2-{v_i}^2}{2d}$$.
